channel.title: Goldman Sachs Insights (unofficial mirror)
channel.description: Unofficial RSS mirror of articles, videos, and podcasts published at goldmansachs.com/insights. Items link to the original Goldman Sachs page.

item.title: How AI is Driving Drug Discovery: Xaira Therapeutics' Marc Tessier Lavigne
item.description: Xaira Therapeutics is harnessing AI to fundamentally change how we discover and develop medicines, shortening the path from lab to clinic for previously "un-druggable" targets. Dr. Marc Tessier-Lavigne, co-founder, chairman, and CEO of Xaira Therapeutics, sits down with Sonia Gupta, co-head of Biotech Investment Banking at Goldman Sachs, to explore his journey from neuroscience to higher-education, the critical role of talent and purpose in building world-class organizations, and a roadmap for translating scientific insight into real-world impact. This session was recorded at the 2025 Goldman Sachs Builders and Innovators Summit on October 16, 2025.

item.title: Long Gold and Long Stocks
item.description: Do investors need a new barbell strategy? Anshul Sehgal, global co-head of Fixed Income, Currency and Commodities in Goldman Sachs Global Banking & Markets, discusses market drivers and portfolio positioning with Chris Hussey on the Goldman Sachs trading floor. This episode was recorded on January 29, 2026.

item.title: Americas First
item.description: The Trump Administration kicked off 2026 with a new, more forceful foreign policy, driven by the so-called “Donroe Doctrine.”

item.title: Poppi Founders: Disrupting the Beverage Industry
item.description: Poppi began as a kitchen experiment with apple cider vinegar and has grown into a leading beverage brand, which was recently acquired by PepsiCo for nearly $2 billion. Allison and Stephen Ellsworth, co-founders of Poppi, joined Vanya Kasanof to share the brand’s journey to becoming a digital-first sensation and a category leader. They discuss Poppi’s rapid rise, the power of intuition and culture, and how a relentless focus on brand and community propelled them from farmers markets to a landmark Super Bowl ad. This session was recorded at the 2025 Goldman Sachs Builders and Innovators Summit on October 15, 2025.

item.title: Productivity Stagnation in the Construction Industry: An International Perspective
item.description: From 1970 to 2024, productivity in the US construction industry fell by 30% while economy-wide productivity more than doubled.

item.title: Implications of an “Americas First” Foreign Policy
item.description: What could more assertive US foreign policy in the Western Hemisphere — driven by the so-called “Donroe Doctrine”— mean for the global order?

item.title: Will Japanese Stocks Rally to Fresh Record Highs?
item.description: Goldman Sachs Research expects a central bank rate hike and US reindustrialization to help boost Japan’s stock market in 2026.

item.title: Inside the Future of Payments: Adyen CEO
item.description: Description: Adyen has scaled from an Amsterdam startup to a global fintech by building every layer of payments in-house and focusing relentlessly on merchants. Pieter van der Does, co-founder and co-CEO of Adyen, joins Clif Marriott, co-head of Technology, Media and Telecoms in EMEA at Goldman Sachs, to discuss Adyen's origin story, the strategy that helped Adyen scale to roughly $900 billion in annual processed volume, and a winning company culture that fosters global collaboration. This session was recorded in London on November 5, 2025.

item.title: Latin America Is Predicted to Post Modest Growth amid Upheaval in Venezuela
item.description: Latin America is expected to have steady though modest economic growth and broadly stable inflation despite US tariffs and upheaval in Venezuela.

item.title: Finding Opportunities in Financials
item.description: Can US financials outperform in 2026 – and where are the most attractive opportunities? Christian DeGrasse, financial sector specialist in Global Banking & Markets, discusses with Chris Hussey on the Goldman Sachs trading floor. This episode was recorded on February 4, 2026.

item.title: The Outlook for India’s Economy in 2026 amid A New US Trade Deal
item.description: Goldman Sachs Research forecasts India’s real GDP to grow at 6.9% in 2026—with policy continuity and a boost thanks to the new US trade deal.

item.title: Stock Market Shakeout
item.description: There is significant volatility beneath the surface of the US equity market. So what’s driving these moves – and where are the opportunities for investors now? Shawn Tuteja, who oversees ETF and custom baskets volatility trading within Goldman Sachs Global Banking & Markets, discusses with Chris Hussey on the Goldman Sachs trading floor.

item.title: ‘Affordability’: Consumer Concerns and Government Proposals
item.description: What's driving concerns about the US cost of living, and can the administration's proposals effectively address them? David Mericle, Chief US Economist, and Alec Phillips, Chief US Political Economist, at Goldman Sachs Research discuss with Allison Nathan. Date of recording: February 5th, 2026.

item.title: Tech Stocks in Trouble?
item.description: Will the US tech giants keep underperforming? Mike Washington, equities sales trader in Global Banking & Markets, separates the signal from the noise. Recorded on February 5, 2026.

item.title: Fundamentals Still Matter: Lone Pine’s David Craver
item.description: David Craver, co-chief investment officer of Lone Pine Capital, discusses the evolution of market structure, the opportunities in AI, and the factors driving the firm’s long-term fundamental-based investing.

item.title: Why Has Productivity in the US Construction Industry Stagnated?
item.description: Construction productivity has underperformed productivity in the wider economy partly due to technological changes, regulation, and measurement issues, according to Goldman Sachs Research.

item.title: Hedge Funds Have Momentum After Posting Double-Digit Returns Last Year
item.description: As hedge funds deliver double-digit returns and unprecedented alpha, asset allocators surveyed by Goldman Sachs Prime Services plan to boost exposure to the group.

item.title: “Still Bullish Gold”
item.description: What’s behind the recent volatility in gold prices – and what could drive further gains through the rest of the year? Lina Thomas, senior commodities analyst in Goldman Sachs Research, discusses with Chris Hussey.

item.title: The New AI Trades
item.description: Goldman Sachs Research’s Ryan Hammond explains how AI disruption is creating large sector rotations even as the equities continue to move higher.

item.title: Which Software Companies Will Benefit from AI?
item.description: Investors have pushed some software stocks lower on concern that AI agents could make them obsolete, but some of these companies may be insulated and even benefit from AI.

item.title: How Indonesia Drove a Rally in Nickel
item.description: Indonesian supply policy is driving the price of nickel

item.title: Tech Comeback Ahead?
item.description: Despite widespread optimism about AI, the US tech sector has lagged meaningfully this year. What factors could lead to a bullish reversal – and which stocks may be set to rebound? Peter Callahan, the US Technology, Media and Telecommunications sector specialist within Goldman Sachs Global Banking & Markets, discusses with Mike Washington on the Goldman Sachs trading floor.

item.title: Board Governance in 2026: AI Oversight, Activism, and Delaware’s Edge
item.description: Board governance is central to how companies earn and maintain trust with shareholders and the public. Larry Cunningham, Presiding Director of the John L. Weinberg Center for Corporate Governance at the University of Delaware, joins John F.W. Rogers, Secretary of the Board of Directors at Goldman Sachs, to discuss the evolution of board stewardship and its importance today. Cunningham discusses the Center’s 25th anniversary and the discovery of John L. Weinberg’s 1948 Princeton thesis on the status and functions of corporate directors. They explore enduring governance questions including shareholder focus versus broader corporate responsibilities, the value of discerning questions, and why one-size-fits-all governance checklists often miss what matters most. This episode was recorded on October 27, 2025.

item.title: Why Japanese Stocks Could Keep Rallying
item.description: Goldman Sachs Research’s Bruce Kirk discusses the implications of Japan’s recent snap election and the implications for the equity market and foreign investors.

item.title: The S&P 500 Is Forecast to Rally amid a Healthy Economy in 2026
item.description: S&P 500 outlook for 2026: Despite elevated valuations, US stocks are forecast to post double-digit gains.

item.title: Global GDP Outlook in 2026: Sturdy Growth and Stable Prices
item.description: Global economic outlook: Goldman Sachs Research expects sturdy growth in 2026 driven by US tax cuts, Chinese export strength, and fiscal expansion in the euro area.

item.title: Finding Software Opportunities
item.description: Is the software selloff creating opportunities for long-term investors?

item.title: European Stocks Have a Promising Backdrop for 2026
item.description: STOXX 600 outlook for 2026: European stocks are expected to rise this year, boosted by German fiscal spending, improving global growth, and investors diversifying out of concentrated US equities.

item.title: European GDP Growth in 2026: Signs of Optimism
item.description: European economic outlook: GDP growth is forecast to accelerate in 2026 amid higher German public spending and an encouraging backdrop for consumption.

item.title: An Optimistic Outlook for US GDP in 2026 as Inflation Cools
item.description: US economic outlook for 2026: Our economists expect solid GDP growth, in part because of a boost from tax cuts.

item.title: A New Way to Trade Emerging Markets
item.description: Emerging market equities have continued to rally in 2026. What’s driving the gains, and where are the opportunities now? Stratford Dennis discusses with Chris Hussey.

item.title: Forecasts for the World’s Biggest Economies in 2026
item.description: Some of the world’s biggest economies are forecast to receive a boost in 2026 from higher fiscal spending, declining policy rates, and a reduced tariff impact.

item.title: Could Value Stocks Benefit from the AI Rout?
item.description: A decline in software stocks has investors shifting their attention to once-neglected sectors with the physical assets required for mass AI adoption.

item.title: Oil Market Impacts from Iran
item.description: Goldman Sachs Research’s Daan Struyven, co-head of Global commodities Research and Head of Oil Research, discusses how the strikes in Iran affect the oil market, commodities, and energy prices.

item.title: Lloyd Blankfein on His Memoir, Risk Management and Leadership
item.description: Goldman Sachs’ culture has long been defined by its core values: partnership, client service, integrity, and excellence. Lloyd Blankfein, Senior Chairman of The Goldman Sachs Group Inc. and former Chairman and CEO of Goldman Sachs, joins David Solomon, current Chairman and CEO of Goldman Sachs for a conversation on his storied career and the launch of his memoir, Streetwise: Getting to and Through Goldman Sachs. Lloyd reflects on his upbringing in Brooklyn, path to Harvard, long-time leadership at Goldman Sachs, the evolution of the firm’s partnership culture, and the critical risk management lessons learned while steering the firm through the 2008 financial crisis and its aftermath. This episode was recorded on February 25, 2026.

item.title: How Will the Iran Conflict Impact Oil Prices?
item.description: Following US and Israeli strikes in Iran, the outlook for energy prices will likely be closely linked to disruptions to flows through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: $120 Oil Ahead?
item.description: determine the commodity’s next move, and how much higher could oil prices rise? Jerome Dortmans, co-head of Global Oil and Products Trading in Goldman Sachs Global Banking & Markets, discusses with Chris Hussey on the Goldman Sachs trading floor.

item.title: GS SUSTAIN: How Rising Hyperscaler Reinvestment Impacts the 6 Ps Driving Power Growth / Constraints
item.description: Goldman Sachs Research raises their data center power demand outlook and now expects 220% growth by 2030 vs. 2023 levels (vs +175% previously).

item.title: New Worries: The Latest Jobs Report and Geopolitical Dynamics
item.description: A weaker-than-expected employment report, and increasing geopolitical concerns, are leading to significant market volatility. How should investors make sense of this environment? Josh Schiffrin, Chief Strategy Officer and Head of Financial Risk for Goldman Sachs Global Banking & Markets, discusses with Chris Hussey on the Goldman Sachs trading floor.

item.title: Will AI Eat Software?
item.description: The emergence of agentic AI tools for software development has fueled concerns that AI could eat software, triggering a sharp re-rating of the software sector.

item.title: Can Software Survive AI?
item.description: Goldman Sachs Research’s Gabriela Borges explains how AI disruption has been affecting the software sector and what it will take for the sector to stabilize. For more, also read the related Goldman Sachs Research’s Top of Mind report, Will AI eat software?

item.title: Why Active ETFs Are Gaining Momentum as Investors Seek New Solutions
item.description: Active ETFs are growing nearly five times faster than their passive peers as investors seek more flexibility than index-trackers can provide.

item.title: Untangling Signals of Market Stress
item.description: Geopolitical uncertainty and a decline in S&P 500 liquidity are prompting institutional investors to hedge against macro risks. How are investors responding to surging volumes and shifting thematic trades? John Flood, head of Americas Equities Execution Services in Goldman Sachs Global Banking & Markets, discusses with Chris Hussey on the Goldman Sachs trading floor.

item.title: Why Korea’s Stock Market Is Forecast to Rise to Record Highs
item.description: A recent drop in Korean equity prices could provide an attractive opportunity for investors amid a positive outlook for semiconductor companies and the market’s history of bouncing back from steep declines.

item.title: Goldman Sachs International’s Co-CEOs on Europe’s Opportunity, AI, and Market Volatility
item.description: Goldman Sachs International Co-CEOs Anthony Gutman and Kunal Shah discuss CEO sentiment, market volatility, and the case for Europe.

item.title: Europe Has an Edge in Developing Applications for AI
item.description: European entrepreneurs and early-stage investors are making great strides in the development of AI applications.

item.title: Emerging Markets: Stirred, But Not Yet Shaken
item.description: Goldman Sachs Research’s Kamakshya Trivedi analyzes the market’s reaction to the conflict in Iran and the case for emerging markets.

item.title: How Will AI Affect the US Labor Market?
item.description: Goldman Sachs Research estimates that 300 million jobs globally are exposed to automation by AI. But the technology will also generate new employment.

item.title: "The Market Is Fragile": John Storey on Finding Opportunities in Turbulent Markets
item.description: While the S&P 500 has traded within a relatively narrow range this year, there are significant signals of stress below the surface, says John Storey, Co-Head of Equities Distribution in Goldman Sachs Global Banking & Markets. He discusses investor positioning, and the potential for “asset-heavy” companies to outperform, in this conversation with Chris Hussey.

item.title: Steven Mnuchin: Navigating Credit Cycles, AI Investment, and Fiscal Policy
item.description: Steven Mnuchin, former U.S. Treasury Secretary and Goldman Sachs partner alum, brings decades of experience navigating financial markets, public policy, and economic crises. In this conversation with David Solomon, Chairman and CEO of Goldman Sachs, Mnuchin shares insights on the current credit cycle, the resilience of the U.S. economy amid unprecedented AI infrastructure spending, cryptocurrency regulation and the future of stablecoins, and lessons learned from managing through the COVID-19 economic shutdown. This session was recorded at the Goldman Sachs 24th Annual Alternatives Summit on October 21, 2025.

item.title: Iran Conflict: How Long, and How Bad?
item.description: The unprecedented US and Israeli coordinated attack on Iran has resulted in the largest energy supply disruption in history, as shipping traffic in the Strait of Hormuz has ground to a near halt and regional energy infrastructure has come under target. How long the conflict could last, its impact on global energy supplies, and economic and market implications are Top of Mind.

item.title: Private Credit Concerns in Context
item.description: Goldman Sachs’ Alex Blostein and Vivek Bantwal discuss the market sentiment, fundamentals, and the outlook for the private credit sector.

item.title: The HALO effect: Heavy Assets, Low Obsolescence in the AI era
item.description: Goldman Sachs Research analysts introduce the "HALO" framework—Heavy Assets, Low Obsolescence—to identify companies that are less exposed to technological obsolescence.

item.title: Is a Further Equity Correction Ahead?
item.description: Are equities at significant risk of a further correction – or even a bear market? Peter Oppenheimer of Goldman Sachs Research separates the signal from the noise.

item.title: The Outlook for Private Credit amid Rising Market Stress
item.description: Despite concern about increasing credit market stress and lending to software companies that may face AI disruption, there are signs that private credit markets are relatively stable.

item.title: Lindsay Rosner: Why There Could Be More Opportunities in Fixed Income
item.description: Bond yields have risen sharply in the past month. Is this move an overreaction – and is it creating opportunities for investors? Lindsay Rosner, Head of Multi-Sector Investing in Goldman Sachs Asset Management, discusses with Chris Hussey.

item.title: Iran Conflict: How Long, and How Bad?
item.description: The Iran conflict has triggered the largest energy supply disruption in history with shipping traffic in the Strait of Hormuz nearly at a standstill and regional energy infrastructure under fire.

item.title: Why Are UK Gilt Yields So High?
item.description: The war in Iran has prompted investors to sell UK government bonds, pushing yields to an 18-year high as inflation concerns rise.

item.title: How the Iran War Is Impacting Investment Portfolios
item.description: Losses in balanced multi-asset portfolios have been limited since the start of the Iran war, but some risks remain. Our strategists recommend a portfolio split equally between assets exposed to innovation, assets protecting against inflation, and assets that help mitigate risks like economic downturns.

item.title: The Business Builder: New Mountain Capital's Steve Klinsky
item.description: Steve Klinsky, founder and CEO of New Mountain Capital, joins Goldman Sachs’ Alison Mass to discuss the macro landscape, his approach to building businesses, the evolution of the private equity industry.

item.title: How the Conflict in the Strait of Hormuz Could Affect Global Agriculture Prices
item.description: The turmoil in the Strait of Hormuz could disrupt global flows of nitrogen fertilizers and LNG that are important for agriculture around the world.

item.title: AI Exchanges: Power Problems?
item.description: As the demand for AI-powered data centers continues to grow, Goldman Sachs Research’s Brian Singer explains the potential sources—and constraints—of power demand growth.

item.title: Natural Gas in Focus: Iran Conflict Could Have ‘Very Painful’ Consequences
item.description: While oil dominates headlines amid the Iran conflict, an equally unsettling story is playing out in natural gas markets that may pose an even greater threat. In this episode of Goldman Sachs Exchanges, Samantha Dart, co-head of Global Commodities Research, explains why the length of the Iran conflict could have a significant impact on natural gas prices. This episode was recorded on April 6th, 2026.

item.title: Sir Sadiq Khan on London’s Global Edge—and What Comes Next
item.description: London’s position as a global hub depends on staying competitive and preparing for rapid technological change. Sir Sadiq Khan, Mayor of London, joins Kunal Shah, co-chief executive officer of Goldman Sachs International and global co-head of FICC, to discuss London’s economic strengths across finance, culture, and innovation—and the potential challenges around artificial intelligence, talent and capital. They explore the London Growth Plan, the city’s focus on transportation and air quality, and how London can build guardrails around innovation while also capturing opportunity.

item.title: Reimagining Biopharma: Precision Medicine, Patients, and Purpose with Takeda CEO-Elect
item.description: Takeda Pharmaceuticals operates at the intersection of deep scientific innovation and complex global healthcare systems, with a portfolio spanning gastroenterology, oncology, neuroscience, rare diseases, and plasma-derived therapies. CEO-elect Julie Kim joins Yassaman Salas, global co-head of the Healthcare Group within Investment Banking at Goldman Sachs, to discuss the promise of precision medicine, the role of AI and real-world data in accelerating development and access, and how she plans to sharpen Takeda’s growth story while preserving its patient-first, values-driven culture.

item.title: Can the China Rally Continue?
item.description: What’s driving Chinese equity markets higher, and are there better opportunities across the emerging markets landscape at this point?

item.title: Big Tech Opportunity: Lee Coppersmith on US Equities
item.description: After a huge one-day rally on the Iran ceasefire deal, is there still room for stocks to go higher? And what’s the best way for investors to take bullish positions on US equities now? Lee Coppersmith, who co-runs New York Equity Derivatives Sales in Goldman Sachs Global Banking & Markets, discusses with Mike Washington.

item.title: Are Technology Stocks Cheap Now?
item.description: Global technology stocks started 2026 with one of the worst periods of underperformance relative to non-tech sectors since the early 1970s. The decline means they are no longer expensive compared to the rest of the stock market, according to Goldman Sachs Research.

item.title: Holocaust Survivors on Hope, Survival, and the Responsibility to Remember
item.description: As children during the Holocaust, Henry and Bernie Schanzer were hidden and moved through a network of protectors who defied the Nazi regime to save them. They joined Sheara Fredman, Chief Accounting Officer and Controller at Goldman Sachs, to reflect on lessons about moral courage, and share why storytelling remains a powerful tool for remembrance across generations.

item.title: Why Aren’t Investors More Worried?
item.description: Goldman Sachs Research’s Dominic Wilson discusses the impact of the Iran conflict—including the US blockade of the Strait of Hormuz—on global markets. In discussion with Exchanges Host Allison Nathan, he explores the market reactions, risks, and the outlook for equities, rates, currencies, and portfolio positioning amid the ongoing geopolitical uncertainty.

item.title: Apollo's Jim Zelter on the Future of Private Credit
item.description: Apollo Global Management President Jim Zelter talks about the unprecedented surge in capital expenditures, the future of private credit, and where he’s seeing investment opportunities around the world.

item.title: Chase or Fade the Rally?: Bobby Molavi on the Equity Bounce
item.description: US equities have staged a powerful rebound, coming back from their late-March lows to hit all-time highs. Are more gains ahead, or should traders look to take profits?

item.title: Prepare for More Volatility?
item.description: Equity volatility could rise in the months ahead — even if stocks continue to rally. Vickie Chang of Goldman Sachs Research separates the signal from the noise.

item.title: Farallon Capital's Nicolas Giauque on Investing for the Long Term
item.description: Managing Partner and CIO Nicolas Giauque of Farallon Capital—a multi-strategy firm founded 40 years ago—talks about the investing opportunities he sees today and his view of the current market environment.

item.title: The path to 2075 — the positive story of global aging
item.description: It is far from clear that the economic drawbacks of population aging are as intractable as they are commonly depicted, according to Goldman Sachs Research economists.

item.title: The Impact of Higher Energy Prices on European Manufacturing Industries
item.description: Higher energy prices driven by the conflict in the Middle East are forecast to reduce European industrial production by 2% by the end of 2027 compared to its pre-conflict baseline, according to Goldman Sachs Research.

item.title: When AI Learns How the World Works
item.description: After a decade defined by systems that recognize patterns and predict text, the frontier of AI is shifting toward models that understand how the world works. The next advances in AI may come from world models, which represent a quiet but decisive change in how machines become intelligent.

item.title: Cybersecurity Firms Show Software Industry How to Navigate AI
item.description: Cybersecurity firms use M&A and well-honed agility to handle disruptive technologies, offering lessons for a software industry bracing for competition from AI.

item.title: Big Opportunities in Small Cap Equities
item.description: After years of underperformance, small-cap equities may be poised for a sustained rally. Where should investors look for opportunities? And can this momentum withstand macro volatility? Greg Tuorto, head of the US Small and SMID Cap team in Goldman Sachs Asset Management, discusses with Chris Hussey.

item.title: The Jobs AI Is Likely to Boost—and Those It May Disrupt
item.description: While some jobs are being lost where AI can substitute for human labor, employment is increasing in roles where AI augments workers, according to Goldman Sachs Research economists.

item.title: M&A Volume Expected to Surge This Year Despite Economic Uncertainty
item.description: This M&A cycle has a few years to run as big deals lead a surge in volume.

item.title: Winning the Right to Invest: 20VC’s Harry Stebbings
item.description: Harry Stebbings has transformed a bedroom podcast into a media and investment conglomerate, leveraging that platform to back ten unicorns. In conversation with Bobby Molavi, head of EMEA execution services and European primary distribution for Equity Capital Markets in Equities, Stebbings discusses his journey from an 18-year-old dropout to a leading venture capitalist, exploring how distribution and brand have become the ultimate weapons in a commoditized capital market. He shares his "science of content," the shifting loyalty in early-stage investing, and why he believes Europe is currently the greatest opportunity in the venture landscape.

item.title: How Warsh Could Shape Fed Policy
item.description: How will President Trump’s nominee for the Federal Reserve, Kevin Warsh, shape monetary policy if confirmed as Fed chair? Goldman Sachs’ Rob Kaplan, vice chairman and the former president of the Dallas Fed, shares views on Warsh’s potential approach to monetary policy, balance sheet, and communications.

item.title: Cracks in Private Credit
item.description: After over a decade of rapid growth and relative calm, the private credit market has come under pressure.

item.title: US Stocks Are Forecast to Rise 6% in 2026
item.description: The US equity market is poised to reach new highs driven by strong earnings growth and massive AI investment, even as geopolitical risks and narrow market breadth warrant caution.

item.title: Robotaxis Are Forecast to Become a $400 Billion Market in 2035
item.description: The global autonomous vehicle industry—spanning robotaxis, trucking, and consumer software—is moving from experimental concept to commercial reality faster than many expected.

item.title: Riding the AI Wave: Megacap Tech and the Fed
item.description: After a divisive FOMC meeting, what policy shifts could lie ahead for the Fed? And what are tech giants’ earnings telling us about the AI trade? Anshul Sehgal, global co-head of Fixed Income, Currency and Commodities in Goldman Sachs Global Banking & Markets, discusses with Chris Hussey.

item.title: Tracking Trillions: The Assumptions Shaping the Scale of the AI Build-Out
item.description: The AI CapEx debate is usually framed as a demand-side question—will adoption justify the spend?—but the size of the investment itself is not a single, fixed number.

item.title: The Energy Crunch Could Accelerate Europe’s Shift to Electrification
item.description: A European push to reduce its reliance on imported natural gas and oil could boost electrification and prove an earnings boon to utilities investing in cleaner sources.

item.title: Will AI Make Markets Less Efficient?
item.description: How is AI changing investment strategies?

item.title: More Record Highs Ahead?
item.description: Are more record highs ahead for the S&P 500? Brian Garrett, head of equity execution on the Cross Asset Sales desk in Global Banking & Markets, separates the signal from the noise.

item.title: Jerome Dortmans on the Drivers of Oil Markets
item.description: Oil prices have fallen thanks to investor optimism that the Iran conflict could soon be resolved. But the situation may be more complicated than it appears, cautions Jerome Dortmans, co-head of Global Oil and Products Trading in Goldman Sachs Global Banking & Markets. He discusses the drivers of the crude oil and refined product markets, as well as some potential trading opportunities, in this conversation with Chris Hussey.

item.title: The Outlook for the US Consumer amid Rising Inflation
item.description: Rising prices driven by disruptions to the flow of oil from the Middle East are having a measurable impact on US consumer spending.

item.title: Cracks in Private Credit
item.description: After over a decade of rapid growth and relative calm, the private credit market has come under pressure as several high-profile defaults, concerns about valuations, and exposure to a software industry vulnerable to AI disruption have fueled a surge in redemption requests.

item.title: Will the Corporate Investment in AI Pay Off?
item.description: While consumer AI adoption has been spectacular, most users rely on free versions. Successful enterprise adoption will be critical for AI economics to work, according to Goldman Sachs Research.

item.title: Optical Networking: The Next Mega Trend in AI Infrastructure
item.description: Goldman Sachs Research forecasts the total addressable market (TAM) for the AI networking sector to increase by 9 times to $154 billion.

item.title: McLaren Racing's Lando Norris and Zak Brown: Building a High-Performance Team
item.description: McLaren Racing returned to the forefront of Formula 1 through a culture of collaboration. Lando Norris, the 2025 Formula 1 World Drivers’ Champion, and Zak Brown, CEO of McLaren Racing, joined Anthony Gutman, Co-CEO of Goldman Sachs International, to discuss McLaren’s team-first approach to performance, the evolving technical landscape of Formula 1, and what has fueled the team’s resurgence. This episode was recorded on March 18, 2026.

item.title: Innovation and Inflation: Twin Forces Reshaping Portfolios
item.description: Technology stocks have staged a formidable rally, elevating innovation from a thematic tilt to a core portfolio driver. Yet this tailwind arrives alongside a recurring challenge — an inflationary environment in which traditional fixed-income hedges have fallen short. Together, these forces are pushing asset allocators toward a fundamental rethink of portfolio construction, as Goldman Sachs' Christian Mueller-Glissmann and Alexandra Wilson-Elizondo discuss on Goldman Sachs Exchanges.

item.title: China’s Exports Are Expected to Slow Before Green Energy Surge
item.description: China's export growth is under near-term pressure as the Middle East energy shock dampens demand. But the crisis may accelerate global clean energy adoption, a business China dominates.

item.title: Securing and Financing the Future of Water
